sections.h: A Valley Carved by a River
sections.body_he: Larkmont Valley is a fictional erosion valley that formed slowly: an ancient river cut its way through layered rock strata over the ages, deepening the valley and widening it as its walls eroded and retreated over time. There is no unusual formation here — just the slow work of water on rock, a common story in any arid, mountainous region.
The valley walls expose hundreds of millions of years of strata — sandstone, limestone, and chert — layer upon layer, from the oldest to the newest.

sections.h: What You See from the Rim
sections.body_he: From the valley rim, where the house stands, the entire floor spreads out below — a fictional area composed for the demo only.
